criterion_scores.name: correct-prioritization
criterion_scores.judge_rationale: SOW is clearly #1 and tied to its being today's only hard external deadline; the deck gets protected time toward its Wednesday EOD deadline. The action list schedules SOW preparation before the 10:00 1:1, obtains signature during it, and sends by 11:30 — well before the noon deadline — then allocates multiple blocks (11:30–12:30, 13:00–14:00, 14:30–16:00, 17:00+) to the board deck.

criterion_scores.name: surfaces-not-silently-resolves
criterion_scores.judge_rationale: Fabricates contradictions/clashes that do not exist in the materials. The candidate labels the relationship between Dana's weekly priority (board deck) and the client's same-day noon deadline as a 'Priority Conflict' and 'Flag,' but the materials contain no scheduling clash (noon today vs. Wednesday EOD) and no superseded instruction — only a general priority and a specific urgent deadline on different horizons. The expense-report assumption is stated, but the invented conflict triggers the score-1 anchor.

criterion_scores.name: reply-quality
criterion_scores.judge_rationale: Both replies are concise, truthful, and give a clear next step (SOW before noon; 2pm cover confirmed). The Ravi reply commits to a countersigned SOW well before the 12:00 deadline; the Sam reply confirms coverage of the 2:00 PM Northwind call.
